reasoning: The 5-year Treasury yield rapidly spiked in mid-to-late March 2026 (from ~3.72% up to 4.08%), driven by a $70B 5-year Treasury auction on March 25 and markets pricing out rate cuts. By Friday, March 27, it settled at 4.06%. Looking ahead to the week of March 30 – April 3 (which ends on Good Friday, leading into Easter on April 5), a few factors suggest mean reversion downward rather than a continued increase. First, yields typically drift lower after the market digests heavy supply from major auctions. Second, volume will likely be light heading into the holiday weekend, making a continued aggressive run-up less likely. Third, the February NFP report was a shocking miss (-92k jobs according to some sources), and if upcoming labor data on Good Friday confirms this softening, it will heavily suppress yields. Finally, the FRED historical and baseline projections point to a slight expected decrease from recent localized peaks. Consequently, I forecast a 42% chance that the April 5 value will exceed the March 29 baseline.
